subject: Systemic fix for dirty-tree git pull failures on chromabook

body.problem: Autonomous processes (meshd watcher, manifest regeneration) modify .watcher-seen.json and transport/MANIFEST.json without committing them. These files stay dirty indefinitely, blocking every git pull --rebase. This caused 6 escalations during Session 91 and required manual SSH intervention to unstick the chromabook psychology repo today.
body.recurrence: This pattern has appeared in Sessions 84, 90, and 91. The cron→event-driven transition did not resolve it — the dirty-tree problem persists regardless of sync trigger mechanism.

options.trade_off: Loses MANIFEST.json from git — peers cannot fetch it via cross-repo. .watcher-seen.json already represents local-only state and should arguably never have tracked.
options.recommendation: Partial — gitignore .watcher-seen.json; keep MANIFEST.json tracked but auto-commit it.

options.command: git add .watcher-seen.json transport/MANIFEST.json && git commit -m 'auto: state files' before every git pull
options.trade_off: Adds noise commits. Could integrate into meshd's pre-pull hook.
Git automatically stashes dirty files before pull, pops after. No script changes, no gitignore changes, works for any dirty file. Built-in git feature.
options.recommendation: RECOMMENDED — simplest fix, zero code changes, handles any future dirty-file pattern.

options.command: gitignore .watcher-seen.json (local-only state); git config pull.autoStash true (catches MANIFEST and any future cases)
options.trade_off: Cleanest: removes the file that should never track (.watcher-seen.json) and adds resilience for everything else (autoStash).
options.recommendation: OPTIMAL — addresses root cause (.watcher-seen.json tracked when it represents local state) and adds general resilience.

body.recommended_action: Option D (hybrid). Two steps: (1) gitignore .watcher-seen.json across all 4 chromabook repos, (2) git config pull.autoStash true across all 4 repos. Handles both the known dirty files and any future cases.
body.scope: All 4 chromabook repos: psychology, operations-agent, observatory, unratified. The config change applies per-repo (not global) so each agent controls its own git behavior.

claims.text: pull.autoStash represents a standard git feature (available since git 2.9, 2016) that automatically stashes and pops around pull operations
.watcher-seen.json represents local-only state (which files the meshd watcher has processed) with no cross-repo value
claims.confidence_basis: File purpose inferred from name and observed behavior

action_gate.gate_condition: none — recommendation for ops to implement

epistemic_flags: autoStash may mask genuine merge conflicts that should surface — monitor for silent conflict resolution in stash pop
